text: Drawing on an exhaustive survey of global referendums dating back to 1793, Dr. Qvortrup and his contributors offer a comprehensive analysis of the historical drivers and political circumstances that lead governments to put policy issues directly to the people. *Referendums Around the World* examines the evolving role of direct democracy, exploring why and when citizens are invited to vote on critical legislative and constitutional matters. By synthesizing over two centuries of data, the authors provide a vital account of the mechanisms behind public consultation and the shifting landscape of popular sovereignty.
